content:
- Self-hosted multi-agent AI platform on Debian Linux server at 192.168.0.78
- Connected to Mac via SSH tunnels (18789->18790, 18790->18790)
- Gateway runs as systemd service (`openclaw-gateway.service`)
- Version: 2026.2.9 (installed via ~/.npm-global, NOT system npm)
- See [openclaw-details.md](openclaw-details.md) for full architecture notes

- Main config: `/home/openclaw/.openclaw/openclaw.json`
- Cron jobs: `/home/openclaw/.openclaw/cron/jobs.json`
- Agent auth: `/home/openclaw/.openclaw/agents/{agentId}/agent/auth-profiles.json`
- Agent workspaces: `/home/openclaw/.openclaw/workspace/{agentName}/`
- Workspace files: SOUL.md, TOOLS.md, AGENTS.md, IDENTITY.md, BOOT.md, MEMORY.md, HEARTBEAT.md
summary: Key File Locations (on Linux server via SSH)

content: Atlas (WhatsApp-facing, chief of staff), Forge (builder), Frontend, Designer, QA, Minerva (research)

- **Cron model routing bug**: Bare model names get prefixed with agent's primary provider. Always use fully-qualified: `google-gemini-cli/gemini-2.5-flash`, NOT `gemini-2.5-flash`
- **Auth profile errors**: Check `usageStats.errorCount` and `cooldownUntil` fields - reset to 0/remove when updating tokens
- **Atlas permissions**: Was too restricted. Now has full exec, fs, web, messaging, sessions access
- **Kimi/Moonshot**: Keys consistently return 401. User needs to verify at Moonshot console.
-

- Location: `/Users/jithendranara/Documents/New project/mission-control/`
- Monorepo: apps/server (Fastify port 5178) + apps/web (React 19 + Vite)
- LaunchAgent: `~/Library/LaunchAgents/ai.openclaw.mission-control.plist`
- 4300-line monolith App.tsx - functional but could benefit from componentization

- Platform: Debian Linux on 192.168.0.78 (home network)
- User: `openclaw` on Linux
- Mac connection: SSH tunnels (18789, 18790)
- Gateway: systemd service `openclaw-gateway.service`
- Version tracking: `~/.npm-global` has the actual binary, NOT system npm at `/usr/lib`

| Agent | Role | Primary Provider |
|-------|------|-----------------|
| Atlas | WhatsApp-facing chief of staff | anthropic |
| Forge | Builder/developer | anthropic |
| Frontend | Frontend specialist | anthropic |
| Designer | Design specialist | anthropic |
| QA | Quality assurance | anthropic |
| Minerva | Research/analysis | anthropic |
| main | Workspace agent | anthropic |

- **Anthropic**: setup-token (sk-ant-oat01 format), NOT standard API key
- **GitHub Copilot**: Bearer token
- **Google Gemini CLI**: OAuth (access + refresh tokens)
- **Kimi/Moonshot Coding**: API key (persistently 401 as of 2025-02-12)
- **xAI/Grok**: API key in openclaw.json (tested working)
- **Brave Search**: API key in openclaw.json

Always use fully-qualified format: `{provider}/{model}`
- `google-gemini-cli/gemini-2.5-flash`
- `google-gemini-cli/gemini-2.5-pro`
- NEVER use bare model names - they get prefixed with agent's primary provider

- WhatsApp (Atlas is the primary contact)
- Telegram

- PostgreSQL, Redis, ChromaDB, Ollama (5 models), ~25 containers total
- Resources: 7.6GB RAM, typically ~4.3GB used, ~6% disk

1. Check `journalctl -u openclaw-gateway.service` for gateway errors
2. Check auth-profiles.json for errorCount > 0 and cooldownUntil
3. Check cron jobs.json for consecutiveErrors > 0
4. Verify model names are fully-qualified in cron jobs
5. Check workspace dirs have all required files (SOUL, TOOLS, AGENTS, IDENTITY, BOOT, MEMORY)
6. Config permissions should be 600

name: UI must be chat-first, not dashboard
description: User strongly dislikes traditional sidebar+dashboard layouts. Wants AI chat agent interface like ChatGPT/Claude, not boxed-up panels.
type: feedback
---

NEVER build traditional dashboard UIs with left sidebar navigation and content panels crammed into grids. User hates this approach.

**Why:** User sees this as outdated, cramped, and lazy. They want modern AI-first interfaces where the chat/agent IS the product, not a sidebar panel.
